content.claim: Unlike systems consolidating rural schools, Hungary historically responded to enrollment decline by reducing parallel class sections rather than closing schools, producing a large stock of small single-class-per-grade rural schools. This structural feature constrains how SEN/BTMN students can be operationally served, as single-class schools cannot separate pupils into parallel tracks.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Historical pattern does not guarantee future local government choices will follow same path
Does not measure how single-class-per-grade structure affects SEN identification or service delivery quality
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
